name: El Charro Barbacoa (Bbq)
recipeIngredient: 2 lb Beef roast (eye of round or Brisket) – cut into 6 chunks
1/4 cup Garlic puree, packed in cheesecloth pouch
1 1/4 oz Pickling spice, in cheesecloth pouch
1/2 cup Green chiles, roasted, peeled, and chopped
1/4 cup Garlic puree, packed in cheesecloth pouch
8 oz Canned jalapeno peppers, thinly sliced
recipeInstructions:
1. Prepare garlic puree: Peel 8 whole heads of garlic by smashing the cloves with the side of a wide knife; the peel will slip off easily. Put the peeled garlic in a blender with about 2 cups of water and puree. Drain if necessary, and store in a tightly closed glass jar in the refrigerator.
2. In an 8-quart stock pot, bring water to a boil. Add meat, garlic puree, and pickling spice pouch; salt. Bring to a boil again, skim scum, reduce heat, and simmer for 1 hour, or until the meat is tender. Remove meat and cool enough to handle. Discard the spice pouch; reserve the liquid.
3. Shred the meat finely with fingers.
4. In a large skillet, heat oil over medium heat. Sauté green chiles, onion, garlic puree, vinegar, broth, and jalapeno for about 5 minutes. Add bay leaf, shredded meat, and remaining ingredients. Simmer until flavors blend, about 10 minutes.
5. Place seasoned meat in a shallow baking pan. Bake at 300°F (150°C) for about 1 hour, stirring occasionally.
